detail: Follow-up to iterate-2026-05-17-move-to-backlog (agreed deferral). Add @dnd-kit drag-and-drop so a card can be dragged to the Backlog column (reuses the new POST /api/external/tasks/:id/backlog) and to the Done column (reuses POST .../close). title: Follow-up: CORS-Test env-isolieren + `npm run lint` reparieren/entfernen (eslint fehlt komplett)
detail:
Zwei Tooling-/Test-Hygiene-Punkte, aufgetaucht waehrend iterate-2026-05-17-fix-resume-guard-survives-reload (PR #33, gemerged). Beide vorbestehend und unabhaengig vom resume-guard-Fix.

(a) CORS-Test env-sensitiv -- server/src/index.test.ts: der Test 'rejects Tailscale MagicDNS Origin in the default loopback-only policy' liest process.env.SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE ambient. Wenn die Dev-Shell SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=tailscale gesetzt hat, schlaegt der Test lokal fehl (1 von 1061); mit sauberer Env 6/6 gruen. Fix: SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE (+ verwandte Vars) im beforeEach stubben/clearen (vi.stubEnv), damit die Default-Policy-Assertions die Shell-Env nicht lesen. Klein (~10 LOC, eine Datei) -- eigenes trivial/small Iterate.

(b) 'npm run lint' ist tot -- client/package.json hat das Script 'eslint src/', aber WEDER eslint als devDependency NOCH irgendwo eine eslint-Config (kein eslint.config.* in client/server/root); server hat gar kein lint-Script. Das Script hat im Repo nie funktioniert (CLAUDE.md dokumentiert 'npm run lint # ESLint' als ob es ginge). Entscheidung noetig: (i) eslint flat-config + Plugins aufsetzen und den nie-gelinteten Findings-Backlog abarbeiten, (ii) auf oxlint umstellen (lief im Iterate sauber als Fallback), oder (iii) das tote lint-Script entfernen und CLAUDE.md korrigieren. Eigenes Tooling-Iterate.

title: Re-open a Done/Closed task — counterpart to Move-to-Backlog
detail: Counterpart direction to iterate-2026-05-17-move-to-backlog, which added In-Progress->Backlog only. Done is a terminal state with no path back. A follow-up iterate would let a user re-open a done task. Semantically heavier: in_progress is not a single settable state (it is derived: awaiting_external_start/active/idle/...). A re-open most likely targets draft (Backlog) so the user then relaunches, OR needs an explicit re-open endpoint. Pin the target state in that iterate interview. Builds on the /backlog endpoint pattern.

title: Serve the WebUI over HTTPS so terminal Ctrl+V paste works over Tailscale
detail: Follow-up to iterate-2026-05-18-terminal-copy-paste (PR #38), user-approved as a separate iterate during the copy/paste interview. That iterate added embedded-terminal keyboard copy/paste, but keyboard PASTE (Ctrl+V / Shift+Insert) cannot work when the WebUI is opened over the Tailscale IP: http on a non-loopback host is not a secure context, so the browser disables navigator.clipboard and document.execCommand(paste) is browser-blocked for web content. Copy works (the execCommand copy fallback works in a non-secure context); paste currently falls back to the browser native right-click -> Paste plus an inline hint. Fix: serve the WebUI over HTTPS so the origin becomes a secure context and Ctrl+V works natively. Likely tailscale serve or tailscale cert for the tailnet MagicDNS name; touches scripts/start-server-production.ps1 + the SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE / bind / TLS path. Deployment-layer change, deliberately not bundled into the copy/paste UI iterate. See conventions.md learning on navigator.clipboard secure-context gating.

title: ADR-103 retirement: split WS upgrade handler out of server/src/terminal/routes.ts
detail:
ADR-103 (2026-05-27) granted a bloat exception for server/src/terminal/routes.ts at 1013 LOC (state=exception, was=grandfathered). The Retirement Plan in ADR-103 names two candidate interventions to remove the exception:

1. **WS-upgrade-handler split (preferred candidate).** The onOpen(_evt, ws) callback is the largest single block in routes.ts (~400 LOC) and where the shared-locks argument is strongest (writer-slot + pause-refcount + attach-count + onData broadcast all interact). Extracting it into a dedicated module (e.g. server/src/terminal/ws-upgrade-handler.ts) would be a meaningful LOC reduction without forcing the per-handler-split anti-pattern ADR-103 explicitly rejects.
2. **Auth layer extraction.** If a future iterate adds task-attach auth (who is allowed to attach to which task), the writer-role decision could move out of the WS upgrade into a dedicated module.

**Re-Review-Date in ADR-103: 2026-08-27** (3 months). The reviewer at that date checks whether the seven-handler / shared-deps-bundle argument still holds. If neither retirement candidate has landed by then, the ADR should be re-evaluated or extended.

**Out of scope for retirement** (rejected in ADR-103): mechanical per-handler split (terminal-spawn-routes.ts / terminal-paste-routes.ts / ...) — that duplicates TerminalRoutesDeps plumbing OR extracts the bundle into a shared module, which is the failure mode ADR-101 calls out as 'moving complexity outward'.

**References:**
- .shipwright/planning/adr/103-bloat-exception-terminal-routes.md (full ADR)
- .shipwright/planning/adr/101-bloat-exception-pty-manager.md (sibling ADR — same template, also has a deferred retirement plan)
- shipwright_bloat_baseline.json: server/src/terminal/routes.ts entry (state=exception, adr=ADR-103, current=1013)

reason: Completed via PR #73 (C5 E2E backfill) + PR #75 (ptyReused prewarm-race fix) + PR #76 (followup: workaround removed). C5-embedded-terminal-split-smoke.spec.ts is on main, drives ADR-068-A1 auto-execute end-to-end (Launch → ready{role:writer,ptyReused:false} → 250ms quiesced WS data-frame; reload → ptyReused:true). Resume-click→no-duplicate-launch deferred per spec — needs Claude JSONL bootstrap which isolated USERPROFILE prevents; covered by 204 EmbeddedTerminal.test.tsx unit cases.
Implemented in PR #77 (commit a662027) — server/src/terminal/ws-upgrade-handler.ts extracted; routes.ts 1013 -> 620 LOC. ADR-103 Retirement Status updated.

title: ADR-103 retirement candidate #2 + Re-Review-Date 2026-08-27 (terminal/routes.ts still 620 LOC, exception still active)
detail:
Follow-up to PR #77 (commit a662027, merged 2026-05-27 as 560b971). ADR-103 retirement candidate #1 (WS-upgrade-handler split) PARTIALLY landed: server/src/terminal/routes.ts dropped 1013 → 620 LOC, but the exception is NOT fully retired because the file still exceeds the 300-LOC default (HTTP route handlers + spawn-env factory remain). Two open items remain on ADR-103:

1. **Retirement candidate #2 — Auth layer extraction.** If/when task-attach auth (who is allowed to attach to which task) lands, the writer-role decision could move out of the WS upgrade into a dedicated module — that would be a meaningful further reduction. Currently speculative; do NOT mechanically extract without a real auth requirement (would be 'moving complexity outward', the failure mode ADR-101/103 explicitly reject).

2. **Re-Review-Date 2026-08-27** (3 months out from ADR creation). On that date, the reviewer checks whether the seven-handler / shared-deps-bundle argument still holds for routes.ts AND ws-upgrade-handler.ts. If neither retirement candidate has landed by then, ADR-103 should be re-evaluated or extended.

**References:**
- .shipwright/planning/adr/103-bloat-exception-terminal-routes.md (§Retirement Status updated 2026-05-27)
- .shipwright/planning/iterate/2026-05-27-ws-upgrade-handler-split.md (PR #77 iterate spec)

title: SmartViewer pop-out: replace new-tab with a centered modal
detail: SmartViewer Pop-out button currently opens /preview in a NEW BROWSER TAB (window.open). User wants a centered in-app MODAL instead. Design: reuse the Radix Dialog pattern (NewIssueModal/ModalShell) - viewport-centered overlay (portal to body, dimmed full-app backdrop, NOT anchored to the pane), size min(1200px,92vw) x 90vh, header with file path + close X, ESC/backdrop close; body renders SmartViewer with its own scroll. Suppress the nested pop-out button inside the modal (thread popOut=false through SmartViewer -> MarkdownRenderer). CLIENT-ONLY -> deploys via client rebuild, no server restart. Do via /shipwright-iterate (small UI -> F0.5 E2E: modal opens centered, ESC closes, SmartViewer renders inside). Keep the /preview route as-is.

title: Deploy: restart prod server to activate PR open/merged badge route
detail: iterate-2026-05-30-pr-card-status merged a new server route GET /api/external/pr-status (gh-backed PR open/merged status), but the running production server (port 3847, node dist/index.js) was NOT restarted (deferred to protect running embedded sessions). Until a server rebuild + process restart, transcript PR cards show the new bubble width but NO open/merged badge (client gets 404 -> unknown -> no badge, graceful). To deploy when embedded sessions are idle: run scripts/start-server-production.ps1 (builds server+client + stop-old/start-fresh) OR npm --prefix server run build then restart the node process. Restart kills embedded-terminal ptys (resumable via Resume button); external terminal sessions unaffected. All other client UI changes are already deployed live.

detail: The campaign lane (proposed-campaigns-board-lane.md) currently has only the per-step Launch-next action. Add a SECOND lane action that fires one command into the embedded terminal: /shipwright-iterate --campaign <slug> --autonomous. Resume-awareness is ALREADY solved in shared/scripts/lib/autonomous_loop.py (_load_units_from drops complete/done units; cmd_init resumes pending; reconciliation from result.json), so the button needs NO progress logic — it just runs the remaining pending sub-iterates in order from status.json. Reuses the same terminal-injection plumbing as the per-step Launch. Phase-2 follow-up to the lane spec.

title: Campaigns lane: a COMPLETED campaign should disappear on its own, not linger until EVERY campaign is complete
detail: The Campaigns lane (FR-01.33) currently hides only when EVERY campaign is complete (or the dir is empty). A campaign that is itself 100%% done still renders as a card as long as ANY other campaign is incomplete -- e.g. 2026-06-02-compliance-detective-realign (4/4, lifecycle=complete) stays visible only because 2026-06-02-hook-consolidation is 0/7 draft. From the user's POV this reads as a bug: a closed campaign should go away. Expected: filter campaigns where done==total (or lifecycle=='complete') out of the default lane view -- optionally behind a collapsed 'Completed (N)' toggle -- so the lane surfaces only active work. Affects server/src/core/campaign-store.ts (or the client lane filter) + client/src/components/external/CampaignLaneCard.tsx / useCampaigns. Reported 2026-06-05 while closing the compliance-detective-realign campaign.

title: [Migration] Track .shipwright/triage.jsonl SSoT - gitignore + GATED GC of ~101-item pile (full runbook + autonomy in detail; mirror monorepo E)
detail:
RUNBOOK for migrating webui's triage backlog to a git-tracked SSoT. Plugin cache is on v0.24.0, all tools ready.

LAUNCH (paste into a FRESH Claude session opened in THIS shipwright-webui repo): /shipwright-iterate change "Migrate .shipwright/triage.jsonl to the git-tracked SSoT, mirroring monorepo sub-iterate E. Follow the runbook in this triage card (gitignore migration + GATED destructive GC)."

WHY: webui's .shipwright/triage.jsonl (~101 lines, REAL curated backlog) is gitignored via the /.shipwright/* wildcard with NO negation, so the committed snapshot diverges from webui's own Command Center -- exactly the divergence the monorepo campaign 2026-06-05-track-triage-jsonl closed.

PROCEDURE (4 steps):
1) gitignore (non-destructive): run gitignore_canon.py --project-root . (adds the canonical block incl. the !/.shipwright/triage.jsonl negation), then scaffold_triage_inbox.py --project-root . (self-heal: strip any stale bare ignore line, keep .lock, add .bak). Verify: `git check-ignore .shipwright/triage.jsonl` returns empty (now trackable).
2) GATED DESTRUCTIVE GC -- DO NOT SKIP THE GATE:
   a. triage_gc.py --project-root . (dry-run is the default) -> SHOW total / droppable (machine-churn) / kept, broken down by reason.
   b. >>> OPERATOR GATE: confirm the dry-run before --apply. <<<
   c. triage_gc.py --project-root . --apply (writes .bak, atomic rewrite, re-validates).
   d. VERIFY the diff vs .bak BEFORE committing -- HARD invariants, ABORT if ANY fails: (i) removed-set is a subset of dismissed; (ii) 0 open/promoted lost; (iii) 0 status-change on any kept item; (iv) churn_merge.validate_triage_text returns 0 errors. (These are exactly what made monorepo E provably safe.)
3) regenerate triage_inbox.md from the canonical jsonl (aggregate_triage.py --project-root .) -> open-count must equal webui's Command Center.
4) commit (.gitignore + .shipwright/triage.jsonl + triage_inbox.md) + open a PR. Do NOT auto-merge -- review the diff first; that PR review is the final human gate.

AUTONOMY (the operator's question -- "can I run this unattended?"): steps 1/3/4 are autonomous-safe. Step 2 is DESTRUCTIVE.
- SEMI-AUTO (RECOMMENDED): keep the operator gate at 2b. You are in the loop ~10s. Constitution-compliant (destructive ops are ASK-FIRST even in autonomous mode).
- UNATTENDED: the 2b human gate MAY be replaced by the 2d hard-invariant asserts (abort on any violation) -- but NEVER skip both. The .bak (rollback) plus the un-merged PR are the safety net. webui's dismissals are real human curation (via the Command Center), so the dry-run MUST be inspected -- by the operator (2b) or by the 2d asserts -- before --apply lands.
- DO NOT just commit the pile un-GC'd: that bakes ~53 machine-churn items into webui history forever (defeats the A+B purpose of the campaign).

REFERENCE: monorepo E spec at .shipwright/planning/iterate/campaigns/2026-06-05-track-triage-jsonl/sub-iterates/E-triage-docs-monorepo-migration.md (in the shipwright repo); reference implementation = monorepo commit e52985bc (PR #160).

title: [monorepo] A5.6 dormant-trigger: add a5_phase_b_activated opt-in (deliberate Phase B is a false-positive)
detail:
PRODUCER-SIDE FIX (shipwright monorepo, NOT webui).

WHERE: plugins/shipwright-compliance/.../scripts/audit/group_a5.py (_check_a5_6_triggers, _Convention, _apply_overrides) + shared/scripts/lib/security_workflow.py.

WHAT: add an opt-in config key 'a5_phase_b_activated' (bool). Read it in _apply_overrides from audit_config.json and carry it on _Convention. When true, _check_a5_6_triggers treats active pull_request/schedule as PASS, while STILL enforcing (a) workflow_dispatch presence and (b) the bare-'on:'/no-triggers failure. THEN (webui): set a5_phase_b_activated:true in audit_config.json (next to the existing g2_stoplist) so A5.6 goes green.

WHY: webui's security.yml + codeql.yml are deliberately Phase B (commit 7196205 'activate Security Scan on PRs + weekly schedule'; codeql d66ab55). The check has no way to record deliberate activation -> permanent LOW false-positive. audit_config.disabled_checks:[A5.6] is too blunt (also drops the workflow_dispatch-presence guard).

title: Campaign/Triage launch CTAs have no running-guard + loop never writes in_progress
detail: Two related gaps in the campaign launch surface. (1) DOUBLE-LAUNCH FOOTGUN: CampaignLaneCard.tsx renders 'Launch (Cx)' + 'Launch autonomous' (and the triage-card 'Start Campaign' CTA) with NO 'a run is already attached' guard — only disabled when there is no launchable step/project. A second click while an autonomous orchestrator is already attached spawns a SECOND orchestrator on the same campaign -> racing worktrees/commits + corrupted status.json. (2) NO IN-PROGRESS FEEDBACK: the autonomous loop (campaign-mode.md step 3g) calls campaign_progress update-status only with --status complete; it never marks the running sub-iterate in_progress. The card CAN render in_progress (CampaignLaneCard.tsx ~L178) but nothing writes it, so the board sits at done/total=0/N with the next-marker on the running step — visually identical to 'not started' for the entire build, then jumps to 1/N. Observed 2026-06-08: campaign 2026-06-08-triage-outbox-delivery launched (orchestrator session 079ad1b5 prepping D1), board stuck at 0/4 with both Launch buttons live. FIX (small, two-sided): (a) loop marks the current sub in_progress before spawning the runner (update-status --status in_progress; status already in choices); (b) card disables/relabels the launch CTAs (and the triage Start-Campaign CTA) when any sub is in_progress / a run is attached. Also consider surfacing loop_state.json / loop.lock as the attached-run signal.

title: WebUI-originated triage appends bypass the outbox: manual cards write straight to tracked triage.jsonl on idle main
detail: Residual of campaign 2026-06-08-triage-outbox-delivery (D4 closed by webui PR #120 + monorepo PR #177/#182): D1 rerouted triage_add + idle-main background producers to the gitignored per-tree triage.outbox.jsonl, but APPENDS originated by the WebUI itself (manual issue/launch cards via the server write path) still land directly in the tracked .shipwright/triage.jsonl on idle main. Every such append (and the residence-derived status flips that follow tracked appends, e.g. the 2 webui dismissals folded as chore(triage) 4a2bf49 on 2026-06-10) re-creates uncommitted main-tree drift -> fold commits orphan on local main (the J2 ahead-count pattern PR #172 solved for background producers). FIX SKETCH: route webui-originated NEW appends through the outbox too (core/triage-write.ts append path, residence-derivation already exists for status events); they become visible immediately via the union reader and now carry the pending-delivery badge (PR #120), and the next iterate's setup sweep delivers them into that PR. Do NOT change residence-derived status flips for tracked appends (by design - a status must never orphan from its append, D1 doubt-review HIGH). DECISION 2026-06-10: file only, do NOT start the iterate yet (user will schedule).

reason: False-positive (code-verified 2026-06-10): the webui server has NO triage-append path — triage-write.ts writes only event:status, never a new item. The cited drift (webui dismissals landing in tracked) is the by-design TRACKED-PREFERRED residence rule, handled by reconcile_main_triage. CLI triage_add is already outbox-routed (this item itself landed in the outbox = D1 working). Nothing to build; diagnosis rested on an imprecise legacy memory note, not the code.

title: Campaign board: migrate consumer from status.json to event-log projection
detail: The WebUI server reads campaign status from .shipwright/planning/iterate/campaigns/<slug>/status.json (server/src/core/campaign-status-json.ts + campaign-store.ts). Campaign planning dirs are now gitignored/local-only (monorepo PR #189 + webui PR #121), so a clone/redeploy has no campaign dirs -> the Campaigns board shows nothing on a redeployed instance (a local working-tree instance still works). Migrate the consumer to project campaign status from the tracked events.jsonl via the campaign+sub_iterate_id event extras (monorepo shared/scripts/lib/campaign_status.py::project_campaign_status, ADR-121). Then gitignored campaigns still surface progress on a deployed board.

title: Phone responsive view (<768px) - iterate 2 of 2 (follow-up to FR-01.38 tablet)
detail: Follow-up to the tablet responsive view (FR-01.38, PR #139, merged d30d8a7). Make the WebUI usable on phones (375-480px). Phones already INHERIT the tablet compact layout (compact band is <=1023px), so this iterate focuses on what still breaks below 768px: (1) sidebar should become an overlay DRAWER instead of inline-expand - a 200px push eats a 375px screen; (2) the embedded terminal needs soft-keyboard / on-screen-input handling for genuine interactivity (the user's explicit ask was 'fully interactive terminal everywhere'); (3) modal max-w-[95vw] clamps now actually bite (<463px) - verify EditTaskModal / ContinuePipelineModal / TriageDetailModal / wizard; (4) list + projects tables need card-reflow or more column hiding; (5) touch targets >=44px; (6) iOS safe-area / viewport-fit. REUSE the tablet foundation (do not rebuild): useIsCompactViewport, PaneTabBar, and the persistent-PanelGroup pattern that keeps the terminal mounted across the breakpoint - see memory project_tablet_responsive_view_iterate1.md. Expect heavy real-browser Playwright E2E, especially the soft-keyboard terminal. Route via /shipwright-iterate; a fresh session is recommended for full context budget during the E2E phase.

title: Single-session design gate: Review-mockups + inline feedback on the paused design card (host the emitted review viewer, not Smart Viewer)
detail:
Single-session design gate follow-up (convergence S5). In single_session the design gate is orchestrator-approve (SS2): the phase-runner emits the mockups + the index.html review viewer and stops, and the Command Center shows a paused design card.

DECISION (2026-07-10): mockups render in an ISOLATED full-fidelity surface — a sandboxed full-bleed iframe hosting the design phase's own emitted index.html review viewer (or a dedicated new tab) — NOT the generic Smart Viewer (which would fight the mockup's own design tokens and duplicate/hide the viewer's built-in feedback panel). "Inline" means no manual export step, not "embedded in Smart Viewer". The emitted index.html already bundles full-fidelity mockup viewing AND the per-screen feedback panel; the WebUI HOSTS it rather than rebuilding either.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. Review-mockups button on the paused design card opens .shipwright/designs/index.html in a full-bleed sandboxed iframe the review viewer fully owns (or a dedicated new tab). No Smart Viewer tab.
2. Submit in the viewer feedback panel writes .shipwright/designs/design-feedback-round{N}.md directly into the active worktree, replacing the File System Access save-dialog/download path.
3. Round number N is computed from the existing round files on disk (robust across sessions/machines), not localStorage.
4. The written file matches the existing per-screen/per-split contract shape so the monorepo Option-B reader parses it.
5. Resume action clears the gate (orchestrator.py single-session-gate --phase design --state resume); the phase-runner then reads Option-B feedback and revises, or finalizes on approve-all.

CONTRACT (round-file shape, produced today by the viewer generateMarkdown; direct-write must emit identically): "# Design Feedback - Round {N}" / "## Summary" table (Approved/Changes/Rejected/Total) / "## {split}" / "### #{num} {title} - APPROVED|CHANGES|REJECTED" + File/FRs + free-text notes.

DEPENDS ON (monorepo, done): design-feedback-round*.md gitignored as transient scratch + review-loop.md documents the single-session hosting (iterate-2026-07-10-design-gate-feedback-gitignore, monorepo #355); Option-B reader + file convention already supported.

OUT OF SCOPE: rebuilding the mockup viewer/feedback form in React; rendering mockups in the Smart Viewer.

Track: webui-pipeline-convergence. Supersedes monorepo trg-ff0b2049 (dismissed; monorepo side delivered).

title: Production deploy kills EVERY project's tsx dev server, not just this repo's
detail:
scripts/deploy-procs.mjs findTsxServerPids() matches `tsx` + `src/index.ts` in the command line (Windows: Get-CimInstance over node.exe; POSIX: pgrep -f). That is machine-wide: a production deploy from one worktree also kills an UNRELATED project's `tsx watch` dev server, and a parallel webui worktree's dev server, even though it listens on a different PORT.

Inherited verbatim from the pre-fix inline sweep (iterate-2026-07-14-deploy-self-kill carried it over for behavior parity and documented the real scope in the code comment); flagged independently by BOTH the external LLM review and the internal code review.

The sweep itself is load-bearing: killing only the port listener is not enough under `tsx watch`, because the watch PARENT respawns the child and re-takes the port.

Fix needs per-PID cwd/repo resolution (the tsx command line carries the entry path RELATIVELY, so matching the repo path in the command line would silently disable the sweep instead of narrowing it). Deferred on purpose: it changes kill semantics, which is out of scope for a self-kill bugfix.
hint: /shipwright-iterate --type bug "scope the deploy's tsx-watch sweep to THIS repo (per-PID cwd), see scripts/deploy-procs.mjs findTsxServerPids"

title: Inbox: answer Claude's mid-run question directly in the WebUI (pty write-path) - needs an ADR, deferred from the WOW campaign
detail:
DEFERRED BY DECISION (Sven, 2026-07-14) — the WOW campaign (trg-3edbc478, sub-iterate A19)
deliberately ships the SAFE half of this: the Inbox surfaces Claude's mid-run question and
offers a "jump to the terminal" affordance, where the operator types the answer. That path is
honest, autonomous-safe, and touches no architectural fence.

THIS ITEM is the comfort half that was consciously NOT built: answering the question DIRECTLY
in the Inbox, i.e. the WebUI writes the operator's text into the live Claude session.

Why it is a separate, reviewed item and not campaign scope:
  - It is the first time the WebUI would actively DRIVE Claude's session rather than observe it.
    "WebUI never spawns / never drives Claude" is a load-bearing architecture rule
    (CLAUDE.md rule 1; DO-NOT #17 pty shell-only whitelist; ADR-067 / ADR-068-A1).
  - The mechanism is NOT obviously forbidden — launch commands already auto-execute as a
    CLIENT-side WS data-frame (ADR-068-A1, DO-NOT #19), so a keystroke channel exists. The
    question is whether the INBOX gets a write surface into it, and under what guards.
  - Therefore it needs an ADR + explicit guards, NOT an autonomous agent's judgement call.

Scope when picked up:
  - ADR: does the Inbox get a pty write surface? Which frames, which validation, which
    task/session binding, what happens on a stale/dead pty, how is it prevented from writing
    into the WRONG session.
  - Guards: reuse the client-side WS data-frame path only (never a server-side pty.write —
    DO-NOT #19); no injection when the session is not the foreground task.
  - E2E: answering from the Inbox reaches the intended session and ONLY that session.

Prereq: the campaign's A19 terminal-fallback UX must exist first (it is the fallback this
feature degrades to).
Context: C:/01_Development/shipwright-webui/Spec/design/2026-07-13-campaign-adjustment-proposal.md (A19)

title: Inbox: answer Claude's mid-run question in place - render its OPTIONS as buttons (not a text box)
detail:
DEFERRED BY DECISION (Sven, 2026-07-14). The WOW campaign (trg-3edbc478, A19) ships the safe
half: the Inbox SURFACES Claude's mid-run question and offers "jump to the terminal". This item is
the real thing: answering it without leaving the Inbox.

CORRECTION (supersedes trg-28af7d90, which justified this with a FALSE premise). That item claimed
answering from the Inbox "would be the first time the WebUI DRIVES Claude's session rather than
observing it". That is simply not true, and Sven caught it:

  - `TerminalKeyBar.tsx` ALREADY sends Esc / Tab / arrows / Ctrl-C / Enter into a LIVE Claude TUI
    (shipped, for touch devices — its own header says the terminal is "undriveable on a phone"
    without them), over the SAME `socket.send({type:"data",payload})` writer frame the user's own
    keystrokes use. ADR-067/068-A1 blesses it explicitly: "no new server surface".
  - Launch/Resume does the same: `socket.send({ type: "data", payload: cmd + "\r" })`
    (useAutoLaunch.ts).
  - The actual architectural fence (CLAUDE.md rule 1 / DO-NOT #17) is about SPAWNING: the pty
    target must be a whitelisted shell, never `claude`. Keystrokes into an existing pty are not
    the forbidden thing.

So the channel is a solved problem. The hard part is elsewhere:

  1. CLAUDE'S QUESTION IS USUALLY A MENU, NOT FREE TEXT. AskUserQuestion is navigated with arrow
     keys + Enter. A "type your answer" box is therefore the WRONG MODEL from the start — it would
     send text into a menu.
  2. BLIND WRITE. From the Inbox the operator cannot see the TUI state being written into.
  3. SESSION BINDING. The Inbox is a list across tasks; a write must land in THAT task's pty, never
     the focused one. A wrong-session write is silent and nasty.
  4. The single-writer-slot model (a stale writer pins the pty) has to be respected.

THE BETTER SHAPE (what to actually build):
Claude's question and its options are structured data in the JSONL transcript the WebUI already
reads. So the Inbox should render the REAL OPTIONS AS BUTTONS and, on click, send the matching key
sequence (arrows + Enter) to that task's pty. Precise, honest, no blind text injection — and it is
what a "guided control room" is supposed to do. A free-text box is the fallback, not the feature.

Scope when picked up:
  - Parse the pending AskUserQuestion (options + which is selected) from the transcript.
  - Render them as real choices bound to ONE task's pty; send the key sequence over the existing
    client-side data-frame (never a server-side pty.write — DO-NOT #19).
  - Guards + E2E: the answer reaches the intended session and ONLY that one; behaviour on a dead
    or stale pty; no write when another writer holds the slot.
  - A light ADR is still worth it — not because a fence is crossed, but to record the wrong-session
    and blind-write mitigations.

Prereq: A19's terminal-fallback UX (it is what this degrades to).
Context: C:/01_Development/shipwright-webui/Spec/design/2026-07-13-campaign-adjustment-proposal.md (A19)

title: Inbox: answer Claude's question in place - options ARE in the JSONL; spike whether the TUI takes a numeric hotkey
detail:
DEFERRED BY DECISION (Sven, 2026-07-14). The WOW campaign (trg-3edbc478, A19) ships the safe
half: the Inbox SURFACES Claude's mid-run question + a "jump to the terminal" affordance. This item
is answering it WITHOUT leaving the Inbox.

=== VERIFIED AGAINST REAL DATA (not assumed) ===
Checked against a real transcript (this repo's own session JSONL, 18 AskUserQuestion blocks):

  IN the JSONL (so: free to read, the WebUI already reads this file):
    - the tool_use: {"name":"AskUserQuestion","input":{"questions":[{question, header,
      multiSelect, options:[{label, description}, ...]}, ...]}}
      -> the full question + every option, structured. The "1. 2. 3." and the tabs in the
         terminal are just how Claude's TUI DRAWS this. Never screen-scrape the ANSI.
    - the tool_result -> the answer is recorded too, so the WebUI can VERIFY the answer landed
      instead of firing and hoping.

  NOT in the JSONL (this is the whole difficulty):
    - the TUI's CURSOR POSITION (which option is currently highlighted). It is ephemeral screen
      state and is never persisted. So relative navigation (arrow-down x N) would be a BLIND
      write. An earlier draft of this item wrongly said the transcript carries "which is
      selected" -- it does not.

=== FIRST TASK IS A 5-MINUTE SPIKE, NOT AN ADR ===
Claude renders the options NUMBERED ("1. 2. 3."). If the TUI accepts a NUMERIC HOTKEY, the write
becomes STATELESS and DETERMINISTIC -- send "2", done, no cursor tracking, no blind navigation --
and this feature collapses from "hard and risky" to "small and safe".

  SPIKE: trigger an AskUserQuestion in a real session, press "2". Does it select option 2?
    - YES  -> build it. Render the real options as buttons; a click sends the digit (+ Enter if
              needed) to THAT task's pty; confirm via the tool_result in the JSONL.
    - NO   -> it needs cursor-state tracking (arrow keys against unknown state). Then it stays
              hard, and the honest product answer may remain "jump to the terminal".
  Also spike: MULTI-QUESTION calls (the "tabs") -- how does one move between questions and submit?

=== WHAT IS *NOT* THE PROBLEM (do not re-invent this objection) ===
The write CHANNEL is a solved, shipped thing. `TerminalKeyBar.tsx` already sends Esc / Tab /
arrows / Ctrl-C / Enter into a LIVE Claude TUI (for touch devices), and Launch/Resume sends the
command, both over the SAME `socket.send({type:"data",payload})` writer frame the user's own
keystrokes use; ADR-067/068-A1 blesses it as "no new server surface". The architectural fence
(CLAUDE.md rule 1 / DO-NOT #17) is about SPAWNING -- the pty target must be a whitelisted shell,
never `claude`. Keystrokes into an existing pty are NOT the forbidden thing. Do not justify any
deferral with an architecture-fence argument: it is falsifiable in one grep, and a reviewer who
falsifies it will discard the real guards along with it.

=== REMAINING REAL RISKS (what the guards are actually for) ===
  - SESSION BINDING: the Inbox is a list ACROSS tasks. A write must land in THAT task's pty,
    never the focused one. A wrong-session write is silent and nasty. This is the #1 risk.
  - A stale question (already answered in the terminal) must not be answerable again from the
    Inbox -- detect it from the transcript before sending.
  - The single-writer-slot model (a stale writer pins the pty) has to be respected.
  - Dead/replaced pty between render and click.
  E2E: the answer reaches the intended session and ONLY that one.

Prereq: A19's terminal-fallback UX (it is what this degrades to).
Supersedes trg-475219a0 (which still implied the selection state was readable) and trg-28af7d90
(false architecture-fence rationale).

title: Converge the bounded-scroll-container flex invariant across the modal shells
detail: iterate-2026-07-14-more-options-flex-clip fixed the More-options clip by pinning [&>*]:shrink-0 on ModalShell's scroll body: in a bounded column-flex container, a child with overflow != visible loses its automatic minimum size, gets squeezed below its content and silently clips it (and absorbs the negative space, so the container never scrolls). ContinuePipelineModal and EditTaskModal duplicate the same scroll-body class string verbatim but do NOT carry the guard. Both were verified CLEAN today (no overflow-hidden direct child), so nothing is broken - this is latent, not a live bug. Separately, TaskCard solves the identical trap at the opposite layer (shrink-0 on the child). Deferred work: extract a shared MODAL_SCROLL_BODY class (or equivalent) so the invariant travels with the pattern instead of being re-derived per container. Behavior-preserving refactor; out of scope for a BUG iterate.

title: WOW-Usability campaign (Campaign A, A1-A16): guided entry + Mission Control + premium paint + Ship's Log
detail:
Umbrella item for the WebUI WOW-usability campaign (Campaign A from the wow-usability-concept, 16 sub-iterates A1-A16, autonomous serial).

Turns the Command Center into a guided, self-explaining control room for technical users who do not want to hand-run an SDLC: an event-log reader layer (A1/A2), the premium Private-Bank paint on the website tokens (A3-A5), the npx launcher (A6), a plain-language glossary + teaching empty states (A7), the Intent Wizard (A8/A9), Narrator v1 + Mission Control task view (A10-A14), and the Ship's Log project home (A15/A16).

Specs + mockups (absolute paths; Spec/ is gitignored so worktree agents read them by absolute path): C:/01_Development/shipwright-webui/Spec/design/wow-usability-concept.md (sections 2 + 5), plus mockup-k2-intent-wizard.html, mockup-k3-mission-control.html, mockup-k4-first-contact.html and mockup-story-walkthrough.html in that same folder. Campaign scaffolding: .shipwright/planning/iterate/campaigns/webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10/ (read RUNBOOK.md before launch).

Ordering: run AFTER the webui-deep-audit-2026-07-10 campaign (A0) per the concept -- A0 repairs the E2E safety net and the transcript/polling cluster that A10-A13 build on. Launch from the WebUI: open this item -> Start Campaign (draft->active) -> the board card's 'Launch autonomous' button, or run /shipwright-iterate --campaign webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10 --autonomous.

title: E2E full-suite flakes on Windows ConPTY exhaustion (error 267) under load
detail: The pty-heavy E2E specs (75-launch-matrix, v0-9-5-replay-snapshot-envelope, 70-a, 36-rename-title, and others) pass individually and in small batches but fail in the full 276-spec serial run on Windows, with the server logging 'Cannot create process, error code: 267' (ERROR_DIRECTORY) on pty spawn. Root cause: cumulative ConPTY resource/timing exhaustion across many pty spawns in one run (possibly amplified by fixture cwd cleanup timing). Windows-ONLY: the Linux CI 'E2E smoke (gate)' runner does not use ConPTY and is green. Deferred investigation: pty lifecycle/cleanup under load, or a cap on concurrent ptys, or staggering pty-heavy specs. Surfaced by A00 (PR #252) once the suite actually ran. Does NOT block merge (CI unaffected).

title: E2E snapshot-replay specs need the task forced to done/replay-only state (isolated stack)
detail: v0-9-5-task-type-matrix (and related snapshot-replay assertions) write a cell-state snapshot via writeSnapshotFor and assert the marker renders in .xterm-rows on WS attach. On the isolated temp-USERPROFILE stack the created task is NOT in the done/replay-only state the assertion assumes, so a LIVE pty competes with the snapshot and the rows come up empty (marker not rendered). This is a FIXTURE-STATE gap, not a needs-live-Claude case (the marker is snapshot-sourced, not Claude output) and not a coupling/stale-affordance bug — so it was NOT quarantined (that would hide fixable coverage). Fix: seed/force the task to done (or otherwise ensure the replay-only WS branch serves the snapshot without a competing pty), following the pattern in v0-9-5-replay-snapshot-envelope which picks an existing non-launching task and passes. Deferred from A00. ~16 of the local full-suite failures are this spec.
